perturbation.original_text: Subcontractor shall be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.changed_text: Subcontractor shall be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work, and EDGE will not interfere with Subcontractor's choices regarding Lower-tier Subcontractors. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirements for following EDGE's subcontracting processes and the clause regarding EDGE's reasonable objection to Lower-tier Subcontractors, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause implied EDGE had some control over the selection and management of Lower-tier Subcontractors. The changed clause eliminates this control, but Section 6B still allows EDGE to terminate Lower-tier Subcontractors if their work is unsatisfactory, which is a contradiction, since the Subcontractor's choices regarding Lower-tier Subcontractors are not to be interfered with.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Work of any Lower-tier Subcontractor is unsatisfactory or unnecessarily delayed, or the Lower-tier Subcontractor violates any of the provisions of this Agreement, EDGE may request Subcontractor to terminate such Lower-tier Subcontractor and Subcontractor shall immediately terminate the agreement with its Lower-tier Subcontractors without any liability to EDGE whatsoever. Additionally, Subcontractor shall, at its cost, properly complete the Work within the required time or arrange for a capable subcontractor, approved by EDGE, to do so.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes EDGE's ability to object to Lower-tier Subcontractors, stating that EDGE will not interfere with the Subcontractor's choices. However, the contradicted text gives EDGE the right to request the termination of Lower-tier Subcontractors if their work is unsatisfactory. This creates a direct conflict: EDGE cannot both not interfere with Subcontractor's choices and also demand the termination of their choices. The original text allowed EDGE reasonable control over Subcontractors, which aligned with EDGE's right to terminate unsatisfactory Lower-tier Subcontractors, but the changed text removes that control, directly contradicting the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be construed as a "Pay When Paid" contract. EDGE's obligation to pay Subcontractor under this Agreement shall not be predicated upon the Customer approving or paying EDGE for the Work.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be construed as a “Pay When Paid” contract. EDGE's obligation to pay Subcontractor under this Agreement shall be predicated upon the Customer approving or paying EDGE for the Work.
perturbation.explanation: By changing this clause to 'Pay When Paid' an internal contradiction arises. Section 4.B states: Payment. EDGE shall pay Subcontractor for Work completed in accordance with the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor, but with the change to the 'Pay When Paid' clause, EDGE will only pay once the Customer pays EDGE for the Work which introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment. EDGE shall pay Subcontractor for Work completed in accordance with the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is a 'Pay When Paid' contract and that EDGE's obligation to pay the subcontractor is contingent on the customer paying EDGE. This directly contradicts the original text and also Section 4.B, which states that EDGE shall pay the subcontractor for work completed and accepted, regardless of whether EDGE has been paid by the customer. This creates a clear and direct conflict concerning the timing of payment to the subcontractor, making enforcement uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: EDGE may accept any nonconforming Work instead of requiring its removal and correction upon the condition that Subcontractor shall pay EDGE an appropriate amount for damages and compensation as is mutually agreed upon by EDGE and Subcontractor. Warranty is not waived under such conditions.
perturbation.changed_text: EDGE may accept any nonconforming Work instead of requiring its removal and correction. Warranty is waived under such conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'upon the condition that Subcontractor shall pay EDGE an appropriate amount for damages and compensation as is mutually agreed upon by EDGE and Subcontractor' and adding 'Warranty is waived under such conditions', the clause introduces a contradiction with Section 17C, which states that if any defects exist within one year of completion, the Subcontractor is responsible for repair unless EDGE chooses another remedy. Now, if EDGE accepts nonconforming work, the Subcontractor isn't liable to repair any defects, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If within one (1) year from the date of completion of Work acceptance, or within one (1) year from the completion of all other work or services and acceptance by EDGE (or within any longer materials warranty period as set forth above), any defects exists or arise, then in each case upon receipt of notice of such defect, Subcontractor shall (unless EDGE chooses another remedy) promptly cause such defect(s) to be repaired or remedied at Subcontractor's sole cost and expense, including but not limited to the costs of transportation, uncovering, removal, disposal, replacement, correction, installation and covering. Subcontractor shall commence or cause the commencement of repairs immediately upon receipt of notice from EDGE and thereafter diligently pursue same to completion or cause the same to be diligently pursued to completion. EDGE shall have the right without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to it, (i) to make such repairs and offset the cost thereof against any amounts owed to be paid by EDGE to Subcontractor or invoice Subcontractor therefore which invoice shall be paid net 30 days or (ii) require Subcontractor to refund the price of the Work not meeting the warranties. Subcontractor will extend the warranty period if Subcontractor has been grossly negligent in the performance of any Work under this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Subcontractor shall not be liable or responsible for any defect in any materials used by Subcontractor if such defect was not due to Subcontractor's acts or omissions.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed EDGE to accept nonconforming work with a payment from the Subcontractor and maintained the warranty. The changed text allows EDGE to accept nonconforming work, but the warranty is waived. This directly contradicts Section 17C, which states that if defects exist within one year, the Subcontractor is responsible for repair. With the changed text, if EDGE accepts nonconforming work, the subcontractor isn't liable to repair any defects, creating a direct conflict and unenforceability of the warranty in Section 17C.
